rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Who is the personal representative of the estate of Alice Anivian? A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 G. and CRC 7.51(e)] (2) If the petitioner is the personal representative of Alice Anivian's estate, the beneficiaries of her estate must be identified and must either receive or waive notice. (3) The terms of the testamentary trust must be set forth, including the powers of the proposed trustee as provided in the will. [CRC 7.650(b).] (4) Notice of Hearing must show the complete and accurate title of the pleading to be heard. Now, the notice shows "and an ACCOUNTING PETITION," etc., when accounting was waived. (5) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 7/30/24, the amended petition was not actually e-filed until 7/31/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WYN WANG LEUNG IRREVOCABLE TRUST DATED APRIL 20, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel may be asked to address the following issues: 1) Is Gene Yue objecting as guardian of the estate of both minor children, as trustee of the children's trust, or both? Gene Yue signed his verification as guardian of the estate, but not as trustee of the children's trust. However, the caption of Gene Yue's objection identified himself as trustee of the children's trust, but not as guardian of the estate. 2) Did Gene Yue, in his capacity as guardian of the estate as a party to the October 2020 settlement agreement, release all claims against the petitioner as trustee of the subject trust, based on the broad release language referring to "all claims and demands of any kind and nature, known and unknown, suspected and unsuspected, disclosed and undisclosed, for damages actual and consequential, past, present and future, arising out of the dispute", together with a Civil Code section 1542 waiver of unknown claims specifically added to the release? 3) Is Gene Yue, in his capacity as trustee of the children's trust who is not a party to the settlement agreement, nonetheless bound by the release under paragraph 7 of the agreement, as assign, transferee, or grantee of any party to the agreement or as a person who may assume such capacity subsequent to the execution" of the agreement? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued late by Judge Stephen Murphy Before the court is Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories. The Motion is DENIED without prejudice for the reason set forth below. Attorney Joseph P. Corey was not authorized to practice law in California on July 3, 2024, when he filed and served the instant Motion on behalf of James A. Carte. As a result, Attorney Corey lacked the requisite standing to bring the Motion. Pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 6125, "[n]o person shall practice law in California unless the person is an active licensee of the State Bar." The term "practice law" or "practice of the law" is the doing and performing of services in a court of justice in any matter pending therein. (Birbrower, Montalbanco, Condon & Frank v. Superior Court (1998) 17 Cal.4th 119.) Persons may represent their own interests in legal proceedings but may not represent the interest of another unless they are active members of the State Bar. (Golba v. Dick's Sporting Goods, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 1251; See also Russell v. Dopp (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 765, 775, holding that an unlicensed person cannot appear in court for another person, and that the resulting judgment is a nullity.) A person not appearing as a party in propia persona nor as a licensed attorney at law lacks standing to make a motion. (Pickett v. Municipal Court, San Diego Judicial District (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 844, 846.) Attorney Corey filed and served Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories on July 3, 2024, during the brief period when his license to practice law in California was inactive. Attorney Joseph P. Corey was not eligible to practice law in California between July 2, 2024, and July 18, 2024. Although Attorney Corey is also a party to this action, he did not file the instant Motion on his own behalf; he filed the instant Motion on behalf of Respondent James A. Carte seeking further responses to discovery propounded by James A. Carte. As such, Attorney Corey lacked the requisite standing to file Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories on behalf of James A. Carte on July 3, 2024. Respondent's request for an award of sanctions in the amount of $5,400.00 is DENIED. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. See Local Rule 14.10(B) regarding late tentative rulings. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen Murphy Before the court is Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Admissions. The Motion is DENIED without prejudice for the reason set forth below. Attorney Joseph P. Corey was not authorized to practice law in California on July 17, 2024, when he filed and served the instant Motion on behalf of James A. Carte. As a result, Attorney Corey lacked the requisite standing to bring the Motion. Pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 6125, "[n]o person shall practice law in California unless the person is an active licensee of the State Bar." The term "practice law" or "practice of the law" is the doing and performing of services in a court of justice in any matter pending therein. (Birbrower, Montalbanco, Condon & Frank v. Superior Court (1998) 17 Cal.4th 119.) Persons may represent their own interests in legal proceedings but may not represent the interest of another unless they are active members of the State Bar. (Golba v. Dick's Sporting Goods, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 1251; See also Russell v. Dopp (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 765, 775, holding that an unlicensed person cannot appear in court for another person, and that the resulting judgment is a nullity.) A person not appearing as a party in propia persona nor as a licensed attorney at law lacks standing to make a motion. (Pickett v. Municipal Court, San Diego Judicial District (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 844, 846.) Attorney Corey filed and served Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Admissions on July 17, 2024, during the brief period when his license to practice law in California was inactive. Attorney Joseph P. Corey was not eligible to practice law in California between July 2, 2024, and July 18, 2024. Although Attorney Corey is also a party to this action, he did not file the instant Motion on his own behalf; he filed the instant Motion on behalf of Respondent James A. Carte seeking further responses to discovery propounded by James A. Carte. As such, Attorney Corey lacked the requisite standing to file Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Admissions on behalf of James A. Carte on July 17, 2024. Respondent's request for an award of sanctions in the amount of $3,150.00 is DENIED. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: POWER OF ATTORNEY FOR WYN WANG LEUNG, DATED APRIL 17, 2018
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION First Account And Report Of Agent; Petition For Its Settlement; For Allowance Of Attorneys And Professional Fees For Agent
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel may be asked to address the following issues: 1) Is Gene Yue objecting as guardian of the estate of both minor children, as trustee of the children's trust, or both? Gene Yue signed his verification as guardian of the estate, but not as trustee of the children's trust. However, the caption of Gene Yue's objection identified himself as trustee of the children's trust, but not as guardian of the estate. 2) Did Gene Yue, in his capacity as guardian of the estate as a party to the October 2020 settlement agreement, release all claims against the petitioner as attorney-in-fact under the subject power of attorney, based on the broad release language referring to "all claims and demands of any kind and nature, known and unknown, suspected and unsuspected, disclosed and undisclosed, for damages actual and consequential, past, present and future, arising out of the dispute", together with a Civil Code section 1542 waiver of unknown claims specifically added to the release? 3) Is Gene Yue, in his capacity as trustee of the children's trust who is not a party to the settlement agreement, nonetheless bound by the release under paragraph 7 of the agreement, as assign, transferee, or grantee of any party to the agreement or as a person who may assume such capacity subsequent to the execution" of the agreement? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. WELLMAN 2004 TRUST, DATED APRIL 27, 2004 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: (1) Court Order Finding That The 2004 Trust Was Amended By The 2017 Agreement To Remove Sunny V.H. Luong And His Family Members As Beneficiaries; And (2) Court Order Finding That Sunny V.H. Luang Is Not A Beneficiary Or Trustee Of The 2004 Trust Due To The Termination Of His Domestic Partnership With Decedent
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/02/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for 06/26/2024 hearing): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) If the settlor's surviving spouse is not a beneficiary of the trust, but may qualify as an omitted spouse within the meaning of section 21610, the surviving spouse must be served in the manner required by section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 4) No trustee notification under section 16061.7 was attached to Exhibit 8. Please provide a copy of the notification. 5) Sunny Luong's street address must be provided by a verified allegation, and direct notice must be given, absent a signed waiver of direct notice. CRC 7.51(a)(2) & section 1204. 6) If Sunny Luong's residence or usual place of business is not located in San Francisco County, please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by Sunny Luong as trustee within San Francisco County. See section 17002. 7) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to locate Mary Luu and Douglass Huynh. Whehter notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (e.g., telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. See CRC 7.52(c), section 1212, and CCP section 413.30. 8) Each charitable beneficiary designated by the trustee pursuant to section 5.3(c) of the trust must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. If no charity has been designated, notice must be given to the Attorney General at its office in Sacramento. See sections 1209 & 17203(a)(3). 9) If the settlor and Sunny Luong registered their domestic partnership with the Secretary of State, please provide a copy of a judgment that dissolved their domestic partnership. 10) Please provide a copy of the settlor's will.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA MARIA REDOBLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/22/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now, an AMENDED petition has been filed. Notice of the AMENDED PETITION must clearly show that an AMENDED PETITION was filed and will be heard. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) The will is not self-proving and was not even properly executed by the testator. Petitioner must provide legal authority for the Court to admit an undated will that is not self-proving. (3) The petitioner was not a named as an executor in the will. Bond will be required unless waived by all interested parties. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The amended petition was purportedly verified on 5/3/24, the same day the petitioner verified her initial petition...?)
